verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70/HSP72) is canonically a cytosolic chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or classical secretory signal peptide. However, it is one of the best-documented examples of stress-induced and tumor-associated surface display: multiple independent studies using surface biotinylation and flow cytometry on intact (non-permeabilized) cells show HSP70/HSPA1A on the outer leaflet of stressed and tumor cells but not resting normal cells. It is also released on exosomes and can associate with the plasma membrane outer leaflet via lipid rafts. CAR-T and antibody programs specifically targeting surface HSP70 on tumor cells have entered development. Cell-death contexts (apoptosis, necrosis) also expose intracellular HSP70 on the outer face. This is a well-documented cell-state-induced (oncogenic transformation + stress) surface display of a canonically cytosolic protein.
